item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its a very interesting premise but youre using too many of these █ black boxes, give either concrete numbers or vague approximations, or just cut that info completely. I think you should also flesh out all the info you have, this is a relatively short article given how much info it contains, give it more meat and thatll really help it out. Im currently at a no vote but ill definitely read any revisions you make. Youve got the basis of what could be an awesome article, it just needs a bit more writing. Good luck!

item.title: A little feedback
item.content:encoded: I think you need to make it more clear that those who manifest are real people. Make it clear that 3402-A existed before their manifestation. Other than that, very good scp!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's definitely something interesting here - people who spontaneously appear, tear each other to shreds and then burst into flames is a cool image. There are a couple of core problems with it though. The first is that, especially for a relatively short article, there's a lot going on in here. People that appear out of nowhere, and then walk down a road. Maybe they suddenly burst into flames or maybe they don't. Then they all fight each other for a bit, rip each others skin off and then burst into flames. Sometimes there's a guy with a mask. Nearby business types are sometimes pulled in from nearby too, and sometimes other nearby people are pulled in and something happens. It's a lot.

The second is that your logs are a combination of unnecessary or sparse with info. Combined with the first issue, it makes the entire article feel very unfocused. The first transcript is largely just a repeat of information we've already been given. The only new information in the entire thing is the line of speech from one of the instances. The interview is kind of tonally all over; it doesn't really read like a guy who has been through this traumatic experience, and is loaded with a lot of filler. Greetings, ums and ahs, the whole spiel at the end about "would you like to forget" (which feels very unfoundationy to me).

The visuals here are good and the mystery with the guy with the mask is interesting (though I wish that was developed a little more), but the unfocused nature of the entire thing is leaving me at no-vote. Some additional notes below.

Notes
– hide block

with an asbestos baffle between

Why asbestos, and not some other flame-retardant material that isn't incredibly dangerous?

a group of █ counties

You've got a lot of these blackboxes throughout the article and they all seem unnecessary to me. They are far more distracting than just including the actual numbers would be.

white-collar employment

You use this term a few times throughout the article and it feels very non-clinical. I'd replace it with something else more specific.

In all cases, the humanoids register ordinary heat signatures, though they do not manifest where any kind of camera, including infra-red, are currently recording.

This seems like unnecessary and leading information. You're telling me something isn't the case when I had no reason to believe it was or wasn't either way. Specifically pointing out something isn't interesting or relevant in this way just feels like a very poor way of foreshadowing something.

display mutually murderous characteristics

This doesn't seem very clinical. I'd just say they display behaviour consistent with the -A instances.

In certain rare cases, individuals not matching the profile will be compelled to join the group, invariably resulting in [DATA EXPUNGED].

Data-expunging like this just feels kind of lazy. Like, you want to imply something big or interesting happened but didn't want to actually work out what it was. If you know what happens here, you should just write it. If not, I'd consider dropping this line entirely.

will preferentially dismantle these bystanders before turning on each other.

Dismantle isn't the right word here. I'd go with "skin", "flay", "mutilate" or something along those lines.

Manifestations in which all subjects expire

You shouldn't use the word "subjects" to refer to anomalous instances; subjects generally has the specific meaning of external actors being used in tests. Say "instances" (or, specifically, "instances of SCP-3402-A") instead.

(0128) Dr. Volk: If you will not co-operate, you will be terminated. Your sentence is only suspended while you are of use to the Foundation.

Eeeeh, this is a very old-school view of the Foundation, where they just callously murder people without thought for not doing what they are told. Obviously there is no canon and you are free to characterise the Foundation as you like, but this line irked me.

//At just before 0300 hours

You're missing the closing italics tags here.

item.title: Credits
item.content:encoded:
Thanks

I am incredibly grateful to the wonderful people below, who gave me an enormous amount of critique and feedback on this skip's journey.

  • BananaRepublic for a hard-hitting, early crit that forced me to really look at what worked and what didn't
  • Wazikamawata for numerous re-reads and a huge amount of detailed feedback
  • DrChandra for additional critique, particularly around the nature and clarity of the story's ending
  • AquaBlack for catching hints left over from an earlier draft that were misleading

Image Credit

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Beautiful_Moon_on_a_back_country_road_-_panoramio.jpg
